articles.title: Is the AI boom eating its own tail? The strange economics of billion-dollar deals
articles.description: Tech giants like Google and Amazon are investing billions in AI rivals like Anthropic, creating a 'Circular Capitalism' loop. Companies fund each other to buy cloud services and AI chips, inflating valuations. While demand for AI is real, these opaque deals raise concerns about genuine value versus financial engineering, echoing past bubbles.

articles.title: Iran Makes White House ‘GO CRAZY’; Trump Shouts ‘WINNING’ For 1 Hour On Repeat In New Video
articles.description: A White House video showing U.S. President Donald Trump repeatedly saying “winning” has gone viral online after being posted as a looping clip. The one-hour repetition quickly sparked reactions across social media, with users debating the intent and message behind the unusual format. The clip adds to Trump’s ongoing messaging around “winning” the Iran war and his claims of progress on major policy fronts, even as critics question the optics and timing of the release amid rising global tensions and domestic debate.

articles.title: India successfully conducts 2nd test of long-range hypersonic anti-ship missile
articles.description: India has successfully tested its long-range hypersonic anti-ship missile (LR-AShM), boasting a strike range exceeding 1,500 km and speeds of Mach 10. This advanced weapon, capable of evading modern defenses and engaging large targets, significantly enhances India's maritime capabilities. The successful flight test marks a major stride in indigenous defense innovation, bolstering national security and self-reliance.

articles.title: Under a cloud: the growing resentment against the massive datacentres sprouting across Australian cities
articles.description: Residents say AI factories with unknown environmental impacts are being rushed into development as proponents argue Australia must ride the data boom or be left behind Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast When West Footscray resident Sean Brown takes his 19-month-old boy to the park, their walk passes an imposing new building cheerily spruiked as “Australia’s largest hyperscale AI factory”, a datacentre called M3. He hates it: the construction noise from its constant expansion, the looming towers and the insistent background hum, the exhaust from the growing array of diesel generators that power the ranks of servers inside. Continue reading...
